sections.paragraph:
ὑποδαμνάω

to master or weaken beneath one, Il.:— Pass., ὑποδάμνᾰμαι (as if from ὑποδάμνημι) to be overcome, let oneself be overpowered or overcome, Od.; aor1 part. ὑποδμηθεῖσα (v. δαμάζω), of a woman, subdued by a man, yielding to him, Hhymn., Hes.: —Mid., ἔρως φρένας ὑποδάμναται Theocr.
